ddls:
CREATE TABLE "devices" (
  "device_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device */,
  "device_name" TEXT /* Name of the device */,
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Type of the device (e.g., Main Pump, Nutrient Pump, Arduino) */,
  "quantity" INTEGER /* Quantity of the device */,
  "voltage" REAL /* Voltage of the device in volts (V) */,
  "current" REAL /* Current of the device in milliamps (mA) */,
  "power" REAL /* Power consumption of the device in watts (W) */,
  "total_current" REAL /* Total current of the device in milliamps (mA) */,
  "total_power" REAL /* Total power consumption of the device in watts (W) */,
  "subsystem_id" INTEGER /* Reference to the subsystem the device belongs to */,
  "parent_device_id" INTEGER /* Parent device ID for hierarchical relationships */,
  "location" TEXT /* Physical location of the device */,
  "installation_date" TEXT /* Date of installation */,
  "revised_by" INTEGER /* User ID of the user who revised the device configuration */,
  "revised_at" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last revision */,
  "last_updated" TEXT /* Timestamp of the last update to the device's data */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_devices_parent_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("parent_device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_devices_type_id FOREIGN KEY ("type_id") REFERENCES device_types ("type_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_devices_subsystem_id FOREIGN KEY ("subsystem_id") REFERENCES subsystems ("subsystem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "subsystems" (
  "subsystem_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each subsystem */,
  "subsystem_name" TEXT /* Name of the subsystem (e.g., AC Subtotal, DC Subtotal) */,
  "system_type" TEXT /* Type of the subsystem (e.g., AC, DC) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the subsystem */,
  "total_current" REAL /* Total current of the subsystem in milliamps (mA) */,
  "total_power" REAL /* Total power consumption of the subsystem in watts (W) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("subsystem_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_types" (
  "type_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each device type */,
  "type_name" TEXT /* Name of the device type (e.g., Pump, Microcontroller) */,
  "description" TEXT /* Description of the device type */,
  "category" TEXT /* Category of the device type (e.g., pumps, microcontrollers) */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("type_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_logs" (
  "log_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each log entry */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the log entry is for */,
  "log_level" TEXT /* Level of the log entry (e.g., info, warning, error) */,
  "log_category" TEXT /* Category of the log entry (e.g., maintenance, updates) */,
  "log_message" TEXT /* Message of the log entry */,
  "log_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the log entry */,
  "user_id" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the log entry */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("log_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_logs_user_id FOREIGN KEY ("user_id") REFERENCES users ("user_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_logs_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "users" (
  "user_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each user */,
  "user_name" TEXT /* Full name of the user */,
  "email" TEXT /* Email address of the user */,
  "role" TEXT /* Role of the user (e.g., engineer, maintenance, admin) */,
  "role_description" TEXT /* Brief description of the user's role */,
  "department" TEXT /* Department the user belongs to */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("user_id")
)

CREATE TABLE "device_revisions" (
  "revision_id" INTEGER /* Unique identifier for each revision */,
  "device_id" INTEGER /* ID of the device the revision is for */,
  "revision_date" TEXT /* Date and time of the revision */,
  "revised_by" INTEGER /* ID of the user who made the revision */,
  "description" TEXT /* Brief description of the revision */,
  PRIMARY KEY ("revision_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_revisions_device_id FOREIGN KEY ("device_id") REFERENCES devices ("device_id"), 
  CONSTRAINT fk_device_revisions_revised_by FOREIGN KEY ("revised_by") REFERENCES users ("user_id")
)
